channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Tehran Dismisses U.S. Cease-Fire Conditions as Israel Steps Up Attacks
item.description: Tehran offered its own terms to end the fighting that include a demand for reparations. Israel, concerned that the war might end before it can dismantle Iran’s weapons programs, plans to ramp up its attacks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Inside Trump’s Secret Deal to Deport Migrants to Cameroon
item.description: In Cameroon, the Trump administration found a partner it could pressure into accepting covertly deported migrants.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Pranav Baskar
item.category: United Nations High Commission for Refugees
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Mark Harris; AFP — Getty, fhm/Getty, picture alliance/Getty, Zohra Bensemra/Reuters, Doug Mills/NYT

item.title: Ukraine Finally Got Battlefield Momentum. Now Comes a Russian Offensive.
item.description: Moscow’s forces are intensifying their attacks in southern Ukraine after Kyiv made rare gains along the front.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:description: Pokrovske, a small rural town in the Dnipropetrovsk region of eastern Ukraine, about 60 miles east of Zaporizhzhia, in January.

item.title: Cheap Drones Remain Wild Card in Iran war
item.description: Stopping Iran’s production of drones is critical to opening the Strait of Hormuz and halting its attacks on Gulf nations. But can it be done?
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A missile and a Shahed drone on display during a military parade in Tehran in 2025.

item.title: Sarah Mullally, the First Female Archbishop of Canterbury, Is Enthroned
item.description: Sarah Mullally was installed on Wednesday at Canterbury Cathedral. Her appointment to the role has been both celebrated and denounced by some factions within the global Anglican Church.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: Sarah Mullally, the archbishop of Canterbury, arriving at her installation ceremony at Canterbury Cathedral on Wednesday.

item.title: Sexual Misconduct Report Leaves I.C.C.’s Path Ahead Unclear
item.description: In a report obtained by The New York Times, a panel of judges found that evidence of sexual misconduct by the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court left room for “reasonable doubt.”
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub and Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: The International Criminal Court prosecutor, Karim Khan, at a United Nations Security Council meeting last year.

item.title: Trump Says He’s Talking With Iran. Iran Says He’s Not. Here’s Why.
item.description: The president has domestic and international political motivations for touting negotiations to end the war. Iran has similar ones to deny discussions.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump has raised hopes that the war with Iran might end soon.

item.title: In Denmark, It’s All About the Pigs
item.description: In Denmark’s election, it was local issues, not Greenland or foreign policy, that counted. That hurt the prime minister, Mette Frederiksen.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman, Amelia Nierenberg and Maya Tekeli
item.media:description: Mette Frederiksen speaking at an election party in Copenhagen on Wednesday. She has been a force in Danish politics for more than 20 years. In some ways, her incumbency counted against her, analysts say.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Are Talking About Ways to End the War: What to Know
item.description: The United States is discussing ways to end hostilities with Iran, even as the Pentagon dispatches more troops.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Wednesday. It’s been nearly a month since the start of the conflict.

item.title: Gambia Says the Island Is Cursed. Migrants Saw an Opportunity.
item.description: Thousands of African migrants hoping to reach Europe have flocked to a remote island in Gambia that local villagers say is protected by a curse.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh and Muhamadou Bittaye
item.media:description: A damaged wooden pirogue used by migrants rests along the shore of Jinack Island, in Gambia, on Sunday.

item.title: A Missile Fragment in a Schoolyard
item.description: Examining an image of children near a missile fragment, which landed near a school as several families were sheltering inside it.
item.dc:creator: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times

item.title: Matt Brittin, Former Google Executive, Named the New BBC Head
item.description: Matt Brittin, the former president of Google in Europe, will become the new director general. Among the items on his to-do list: handling a lawsuit from President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: The former head of Google in Europe, Matt Brittin, has been named as the new leader of the BBC.

item.title: Two Men Arrested in Attack on Jewish Charity Ambulances in London
item.description: The police said the men, aged 45 and 47, were accused of arson with intent to endanger life after the attack on Monday in Golders Green.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.media:description: Two burned-out ambulances a day after the arson attack in the Golders Green neighborhood of north London.

item.title: For Australia’s Farmers, Fuel Crisis Comes at ‘Worst Possible Time’
item.description: Their troubles could cascade across Asia because they export a majority of their products.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A seeder sowing wheat at a farm in Bencubbin in Western Australia last year.

item.title: Chinese Education Influencer’s Sudden Death Prompts Grief, and Reflection
item.description: The influencer, Zhang Xuefeng, was known for no-nonsense, some said cynical, advice about how to win in China’s educational rat race. He died at 41.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:credit: Feature China/Future Publishing, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Zhang Xuefeng during a lecture at a university in the central city of Zhengzhou in 2017. He rose to fame for his advice on how to succeed in a hypercompetitive environment.

item.title: Rescuers Work to Save a 32-Foot Humpback Whale Trapped in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The whale had been stuck for days in shallow waters of the Baltic Sea, as rescuers prepared to make another attempt to free it.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.media:credit: Sea Shepherd, via Agence France-Presse - Getty Image
item.media:description: A photograph released by Sea Shepherd Deutschland showing a stranded humpback whale off the Baltic Sea coast of Timmendorfer Strand near Lübeck, northern Germany, on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Threats to Europe Put Its Leaders in a Double Bind Over Iran
item.description: European politicians risk angering their voters if they join America’s war. Yet they could also face domestic upheaval if they take no action to reopen shipping routes that Iran has blocked and ease an energy crisis.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald Trump disembarking from Air Force One in Maryland on Monday.

item.title: How U.S.A.I.D. Birth Control Meant for Africa Was Ruined
item.description: The Trump administration had options for offloading contraceptives once destined for Africa, a newly obtained memo shows. Instead, it has let them collect dust and go bad.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Stephanie Nolen
item.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
item.media:description: A warehouse in Geel, Belgium, where contraceptives have been stranded since U.S.A.I.D. was defunded and dismantled last year.

item.title: Republicans in Congress Fret Over Trump Administration’s Handling of Iran War
item.description: G.O.P. lawmakers who have given the Trump administration wide latitude to wage war with no congressional input are growing frustrated as officials offer little detail about ground troops, cost or timeline.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison, Megan Mineiro and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We will not sacrifice American lives for the same failed foreign policies,” Representative Nancy Mace of South Carolina said on Wednesday.

item.title: Canada’s Supreme Court Hears Case on Ability to Suspend Constitutional Rights
item.description: Quebec’s ban on religious symbols — and a measure that suspends constitutional rights — are being tested in a case with far-reaching repercussions.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:description: The Supreme Court of Canada in Ottawa has devoted four days this week to hearings on the notwithstanding clause, which allows governments to pass laws that suspend rights in the Constitution’s Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday
item.description: The fighting continued while conditions for a possible cease-fire were debated.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: A damaged home in an apartment building in Bnei Brak, Israel, on Wednesday.

item.title: Asia Is Getting Crushed Between Oil Prices and the Dollar
item.description: From India to Southeast Asia to South Korea, currencies are crumbling as governments race to secure fuel that is priced in American money.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli, Choe Sang-Hun, Sui-Lee Wee and Jason Gutierrez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People lining up to refuel at a gas station in Ahmedabad in India on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Rejects Vote to Recognize Slavery as a ‘Crime Against Humanity’
item.description: The United Nations resolution was led by the president of Ghana. Israel and Argentina also voted against it.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: President John Mahama of Ghana during the U.N. General Assembly last year. Ghana introduced a U.N. resolution to recognize the trans-Atlantic slave trade as the “gravest crime against humanity” this week.

item.title: Airport Chaos
item.description: American airports have become a symbol of government dysfunction, and the spring travel season is just around the corner.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Circulates Iran Peace Plan While Sending Troops to the Middle East
item.description: The 2,000 paratroopers heading to the region may give President Trump more leverage in negotiations, but they also leave him with the option of doubling down on military force.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump in the Oval Office on Tuesday.

item.title: Boubacar Ould Messaoud, Leader in an Antislavery Fight, Dies at 80
item.description: Despite being imprisoned and harassed, he helped found an organization to combat slavery in his West African nation of Mauritania.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Boubacar Messaoud in 2002. “In my estimation, eradicating slavery is my duty, and I would sacrifice my life for it,” he said.

item.title: Pakistan’s Army Chief Uses Relationship With Trump to Foster Talks with Iran
item.description: Syed Asim Munir’s role demonstrates Islamabad’s acute exposure to regional instability and newly found geopolitical relevance.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Syed Asim Munir, Pakistan’s army chief, in 2022.

item.title: Iran’s Attacks Force U.S. Troops to Work Remotely
item.description: Iran has severely damaged several American military bases in the Middle East, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A satellite image of Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar in February.

item.title: Republicans Block Democrats’ Push for Public Testimony on Iran War
item.description: Representative Brian Mast, a Republican and chairman of the Foreign Affairs Committee, said the closed-door war briefings were enough.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Gregory Meeks last month on Capitol Hill. On Wednesday he sought to question Trump administration officials about the war.

item.title: Who Is Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf?
item.description: A brigadier general and speaker of Parliament, Mr. Ghalibaf, one of the leaders of Iran’s war effort, has emerged as a potential contact point in any peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf in 2024, after registering as a candidate in Iran’s presidential election.

item.title: Israel Races to Hit Iran Hard While It Still Can, Officials Say
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu gave the order on Tuesday for the military to accelerate its attacks, with a 48-hour deadline, four people briefed on the matter said.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Ronen Bergman, Natan Odenheimer and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Workers and firefighters at an apartment building hit by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Iranians Reject Trump’s Offer for Cease-Fire but Signal Openness to Talks
item.description: Iran does not want a momentary pause in the fighting, fearing that Israel and the United States would use that opportunity to beef up their forces before resuming strikes.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Shashank Bengali and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Wednesday. Iranian officials said they were considering meeting U.S. negotiators in Islamabad, Pakistan.

item.title: Israel Keeps Up Strikes in Lebanon as Hezbollah Signals Defiance
item.description: There were few signs that either side was prepared to step back from the fighting. Israel said it struck an area south of Beirut and issued new evacuation warnings.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Israeli strike in Qlaileh in southern Lebanon on Wednesday.

item.title: Humans Had Dogs Before They Had Farming, Ancient DNA Confirms
item.description: New research pushes the first genetic evidence of dogs back by 5,000 years and suggests that hunter-gatherer groups may have acquired dogs from one another.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes

item.title: Iraq Summons U.S. Diplomat After Attack on Military Base
item.description: The attack came a day after a separate strike on the same base, which hosts units viewed by the United States as allies of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The coffin of an Iraqi soldier killed in an airstrike on an Iraqi base on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Had His Eye on China, Then Plunged Into a New Mideast War
item.description: The Trump administration said the Middle East would “recede” in importance as the China challenge took priority. But the president started the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A military parade in Beijing last year. Three American presidents have entered office vowing to focus resources on the challenges posed by China. Each instead turned to crises in other parts of the world.

item.title: NATO’s ‘Trump Whisperer’ Faces Blowback Over Support for Iran War
item.description: Critics say Mark Rutte, the alliance’s secretary general, has gone beyond his remit by openly supporting a war that does not directly involve Europe’s collective defense.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger and Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mark Rutte, the secretary general of NATO, has supported President Trump’s plan to go to war alongside Israel against Iran.

item.title: Air Canada C.E.O. Draws Scorn for Delivering Condolences Only in English
item.description: The lack of French in Michael Rousseau’s speech about the deadly collision at LaGuardia Airport reignited a debate over linguistic inclusivity in Canada.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:credit: Nick Lachance/Toronto Star, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Air Canada chief executive Michael Rousseau in 2024.

item.title: Four Takeaways From Denmark’s Election
item.description: A desire for change and a fractured political landscape left Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen ahead, but with a tough road to securing a new term.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli
item.media:credit: Mads Claus Rasmussen/Ritzau Scanpix, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen at an election celebration in Copenhagen early Wednesday morning. Her party won the most votes, with 22 percent.

item.title: An American in Russia Is Linked to Neo-Nazi Terror Cells Across Europe
item.description: F.B.I. agents thought they had weakened an online hate group known as the Base. A string of European terrorism cases indicates it has resurged.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman and Lizzie Dearden

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Elian Peltier, Adam Rasgon, Abdi Latif Dahir and David E. Sanger

item.title: Plan to Disarm Hamas in Gaza Is Detailed at U.N.
item.description: A Board of Peace member said the most dangerous weapons would be collected first. He linked compliance with disarmament to reconstruction beginning in the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hamas fighters in Gaza City in November.

item.title: Iran Says ‘Non-Hostile’ Ships Can Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ships with no ties to Israel or the United States would be allowed to pass, the government said, but it was unclear if any vessels would try.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships in the Gulf, near the Strait of Hormuz, seen from the United Arab Emirates earlier this month.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: The U.S. was said to have sent Iran a peace plan via Pakistan on a day that the Iranians fired a torrent of missiles across the region.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Emergency workers inspecting the site of a strike in a residential area of the northwestern city of Tabriz, Iran, on Tuesday.

item.title: After Standing Up to Trump Over Greenland, Denmark’s Prime Minister Leads in Election
item.description: Mette Frederiksen’s party fell far short of a majority, but analysts say she is still in the best position to form a new government.
item.dc:creator: Maya Tekeli and Jeffrey Gettleman
item.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
item.media:description: Denmark’s prime minister, Mette Frederiksen. Her party came in first in Tuesday’s election, but won less than a quarter of the vote.

item.title: Iran Signals Resilience With Volley of Missiles Across Middle East
item.description: The barrage continued as officials said the U.S. had sent Iran a 15-point plan to end the war. But Iranian officials have publicly denied that Washington and Tehran are talking directly.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Gabby Sobelman, Erica L. Green and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gathering around a fragment of a missile that landed in an orchard near the Palestinian village of Hares in the West Bank on Tuesday.

item.title: Asia’s Energy Crisis Stings
item.description: The energy crisis unleashed by the war in Iran may already be worse than the oil shocks of the 1970s. Here’s how it’s upending lives.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

item.title: Russia Launches Daytime Attack on Ukraine With Over 500 Drones
item.description: The assault, which came after overnight strikes across the country, was one of the largest of the war, the Ukrainian authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

item.title: Pakistan Offers to Host Talks Between Iran and the United States
item.description: Pakistan, which has cultivated ties with both Washington and Tehran, sees a diplomatic opening to intervene in the war in Iran, its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, right, with President Trump during last year’s Gaza peace summit in Egypt.

item.title: U.S. Said to Have Sent Iran a 15-Point Plan to End the Middle East War
item.description: The 15-point plan was delivered via Pakistan, whose army chief has emerged as the key interlocutor between the United States and Iran, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An apartment shattered by an airstrike in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: High Oil and Gas Prices Could Outlast Trump’s War With Iran
item.description: While the president has promised rapid relief, Americans could feel the financial sting of the conflict for some time after it ends.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Even when the conflict ends, it may be weeks or months before American families and businesses see a true break in spiraling energy costs.

item.title: Lebanon Expels Iranian Envoy as Rift With Tehran Deepens
item.description: The decision by Lebanon’s foreign ministry has heightened fears of internal instability. Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed militant group and political party, was quick to condemn the move.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mohammad Reza Shibani, Iran’s ambassador to Beirut, has been ordered to leave Lebanon by the end of the week.

item.title: Investigators Seek Answers in Attacks on Jewish Sites in Europe
item.description: Attacks on schools and property in several countries have Jewish communities on edge, amid suspicions that Iran is behind the violence.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman and Lizzie Dearden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Belgian military personnel guarding a synagogue in Antwerp on Monday.

item.title: Costa Rica Agrees to Take Migrants Deported by the Trump Administration
item.description: The agreement is part of President Trump’s efforts to find governments willing to accept people who have been detained in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:description: Then-Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem with President Rodrigo Chaves Robles of Costa Rica during a news conference last year in San José, Costa Rica.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Seemed Unbeatable. A Referendum Defeat Has Dented Her Aura.
item.description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni had led one of Italy’s most stable postwar governments. Now she’s under pressure after failing to convince Italians to back a judicial overhaul.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in Paris in January. Voters on Monday rejected her plan to overhaul Italy’s judiciary.

item.title: Qatar Is Not Mediating U.S.-Iran Talks, Foreign Minister Says
item.description: Qatar’s Foreign Ministry warned that the regional security system has been shattered, as the war has destroyed trust between Gulf countries and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Majed al-Ansari, Qatar’s Foreign Ministry spokesman, said the country isn’t involved with mediation talks.

item.title: The U.S. Military Said It Helped Bomb a Drug Camp in Ecuador. It Was a Dairy Farm.
item.description: The Times visited a village where the United States and Ecuador said they destroyed an armed group’s training camp. Residents said it was actually a dairy farm.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, José María León Cabrera, Annie Correal, Eric Schmitt and Federico Rios
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The remains of a dairy cattle farm in San Martín that was burned and bombed by the Ecuadorean military.

item.title: Reopening the Strait of Hormuz will be difficult without a deal with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Agnes Chang, Adina Renner and Samuel Granados

item.title: Why President Trump Has a Big Oil Problem
item.description: Our national security correspondent David E. Sanger looks at President Trump’s trouble handling retaliatory attacks by Iran that have largely choked off the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Coleman Lowndes, Laura Salaberry, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Alexandra Ostasiewicz and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Russian Oil Shipment Puts Focus on Kremlin Spy Outpost in Cuba
item.description: Moscow may be challenging President Trump’s effort to choke Cuba’s economy. China also has suspected listening posts on the island.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Mr. Trump has spoken of using his economic leverage to mount a “friendly takeover” of Cuba and is pressing for the ouster of its president, Miguel Díaz-Canel.
